title: Boat Rental in Hong Kong: Best Charter Choices for 2026
html:
Hong Kong offers some of the world's most dramatic boating, with charter prices starting around HKD 3,500 for a half-day motorboat from Aberdeen Harbour. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners so you can rent a boat that matches your group size and budget.

Where to set off in Hong Kong

Most charters depart from the main marinas on Hong Kong Island and the New Territories. Aberdeen Harbour is the busiest hub, home to hundreds of junks and modern craft. Gold Coast Marina in Tuen Mun offers direct access to the western islands. Wong Shek Pier in Sai Kung leads to the eastern geoparks. The Central Pier and Causeway Bay Typhoon Shelter are convenient for short trips to the harbour. Stanley Bay provides a sheltered launch point for southern routes.

From these departure points you can reach the outlying islands in 15 to 45 minutes. The Sai Kung peninsula and its volcanic rock formations are a 30-minute cruise from Wong Shek Pier.

Which boat to rent

BoatHire24 lists a wide range of craft for every occasion. The following options suit the most common group sizes and budgets.

  1. Traditional junk best for large parties with onboard kitchen and shaded deck, carries up to 40 guests, from HKD 8,000 half-day.
  2. Speedboat best for watersports and quick transfers, carries up to 10 guests, from HKD 3,500 half-day. One example is this speedboat, a 28ft Axopar ideal for exploring remote bays.
  3. Sailing yacht best for a relaxed day with wind power, carries up to 12 guests, from HKD 6,000 half-day.
  4. Motor cruiser best for families wanting indoor and outdoor space, carries up to 20 guests, from HKD 10,000 full-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Hong Kong?

Half-day rentals start around HKD 3,500 for a small motorboat and go up to HKD 30,000 for a crewed superyacht.

  • Shared tour per person: HKD 300, 600, typically on a junk with 20 other guests.
  • Small motorboat half-day private: HKD 3,500, 7,000, carries up to 10 guests.
  • Catamaran full-day private: HKD 15,000, 25,000, carries up to 30 guests.
  • Crewed yacht with skipper and host: HKD 20,000, 30,000 full-day, includes fuel and lunch.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Hong Kong?

For any motorboat over one horsepower you need a valid local Pleasure Vessel Operator Certificate or an equivalent international licence.

Most charter operators require proof of competence and will ask for a copy of your licence before departure. If you do not hold a licence you can hire a licensed skipper for an additional fee, typically HKD 800, 1,500 per half-day. Book through BoatHire24 to compare verified boat listings and confirm specific licence requirements with each owner.

Local tip: book a boat with a local skipper if you want to discover hidden coves like those at Ninepin Island or Double Haven, which are often inaccessible from shore.

When is the best time to rent a boat in Hong Kong?

High season runs from October to April when the northeast monsoon brings settled blue skies and average temperatures of 20, 28°C. Summer months from May to September are hotter and humid with a higher chance of tropical storms, but offer lower charter prices.

At a glance

  • Price range: HKD 3,500, 30,000 per half/full day
  • Peak months: October to April
  • Trip length: half-day (4, 5 hours) or full-day (8, 9 hours)
  • Capacity: 6, 40 guests depending on boat type
  • Currency: Hong Kong Dollar (HKD)
  • Licence required: for most motorboats unless skipper is hired

Questions about boat rental in Hong Kong

Can I rent a boat in Hong Kong without a licence?

You can if you hire a professional skipper who holds a valid licence, which is included in many charter packages for an extra fee.

What is the best island to visit by charter boat in Hong Kong?

The outlying islands of Cheung Chau, Lamma, and Tai O offer different landscapes and are popular day trips, while the Sai Kung islands like Sharp Island are known for clear water and snorkelling.

How far can I travel in a half-day charter in Hong Kong?

A half-day trip comfortably covers 15, 20 nautical miles from the departure point, enough to reach outlying islands and return with time for swimming.

Is it safe to charter a boat in Hong Kong?

All boats listed on BoatHire24 are verified for safety equipment and insurance, and skippers are licensed by the Hong Kong Marine Department.

Whether you are planning a family outing or a corporate event, browse the listings on BoatHire24 to find the ideal boat for your budget and itinerary. If you own a boat in Hong Kong, you can also become a host and earn income by listing your craft on the platform.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Hong Kong and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
